Now, here's the good news. In terms of tonality and mastering that art of sounding sharp, enthusiastic, and being an expert at an ability to sort of pop it out there literally in the first four seconds of a combination, or even less time than that when you're in person, the good news is it's an entirely learnable skill. It's not something that either you're born with or you're not born with and you can never learn. Yeah, like listen, me, myself, I happen to be born with really, really good on target tonality. You know, I just instinctively know kind of went to sort of, you know, put on the right tone, raise my voice, lower my voice, how to capture someone's imagination and hold it. But over the years, I've taught this skill in this very specific way, which you're going to learn in a moment here, of actually how to break out these tonalities anytime you want. You see, here's the thing, you might be saying, oh my God, there's, you know, this tonality is how am I going to ever learn them? It just, you know, seems like it's so much information. Guess what? You already are using them. You just don't know it. You see, the difference is that I'm consciously aware of when need to pull a tonality out of the bag, use it to make a point, and when to use another one to make a different point here, you've had times in your life where you've sounded absolutely certain or absolutely enthusiastic or you told someone a secret and you lowered your voice. The problem is, is you're doing it unconsciously. You don't do it on purpose. So what happens is in certain days when you're really on and you know, feeling in the flow, yeah, your tonalities are boom, spot on. But many other days when you're not quite in state, you're not at the best you can be, your tonality simply disappeared and vanished. And you watch your sales results vanish. So again, this is an entirely learnable skill. And I always talk about this thing called charisma. You know, what is charisma? Is this, is it like this, this magic gift that's only for the select few? He'll say, Oh, you know, he's got charisma. She doesn't have charisma, whatever, right? What a load of crap. Charisma of all things is a learnable skill. And I teach it to people with the straight line every single day. And you with the time I done will be a master of charisma. You know that charisma, you know, misunderstood word charisma, you know, charisma is like, you know, oh, no, she's got charisma. He doesn't have charisma. What a load of crap. Anybody can have charisma. It's made up of three things. Number one, it's the effective use of tonality to be able to say the right tone. Now, you don't get the right point across with your tonality to appropriate body language. How do you shake hands? How close do you stand to someone? How do you look in their eyes? The firmness they do that great. So it's tonality. Number two, body language. And number three is just not saying stupid things to people because you say something stupid. They're like, you know, get away from me, you know, but think about it with charisma. You know, there's people who are charismatic and there's people who look repulsive. Typically, when you find someone who repulses you, it's not so much what they say. It's their body language. It's how they say it's something icky about them, right? And it's the sort of unconscious feeling you get and it's body language and tonality. It's not the words, typically. Think about it for a second. You know, that someone that walks into a party and you're not even in a sales setting, right? And they start saying ridiculous things. What happens? We're like, we get repelled. Someone comes in and they're boring and then they're dull and their voice is monotone. What do you do? You tune out. Someone comes in, they stand too close to you and they invade you. What do you do? You walk away. Someone that has charisma, they master that ability to just capture you with words, the body language, okay, and saying them in a way that just keeps you hanging on. That's charisma and it's an entirely learnable skill which you will master in short order. Now, not that I want to keep beating on this idea of charisma like a dead horse here, but there's one more point still that I have to explain to you because it's so crucial in terms of sales. Now, remember, charisma is these three things. Tonality, body language, and not saying stupid things. The problem that most salespeople have is that they can speak intelligently about their product, about themselves, about their company, maybe about life itself. They can speak intelligently for maybe three, four, or five minutes. The good ones maybe seven or eight minutes. But when the sale goes past that seven or eight minute mark like most sales do, they start running out of intelligent things to say and they start saying that stupid bleep. You get it? The words stop making sense. They say something that just doesn't make sense at all. What happens is they break that charisma, that magnetic field breaks down, they fall out of rapport and bam, now they have to work twice as hard to get back into rapport once again. This is why it's so important with the straight line. Again, I'm going to really get into this in ultimate detail when we talk about the script, about learning what I call language patterns, having these sort of bite-sized chunks of words that you can put together and say over and over again, slightly differently each time, but they just sound good. They perceive you to be as sharp, enthusiastic, expert in your field. It doesn't even matter if they don't even really add up to that much, but there's sort of these filler words and phrases that allow you to just no matter what seem like you're right on the ball. You cannot master tonality by putting on headphones and falling asleep and waking up with the knowledge. The only way you could really master tonality is through practicing and practicing and drilling and drilling again and again and again. This is one of those things where practice makes perfect and also practice even this much of it yields massive results right away.
